hero_summary: Suznal is not a strongly standardized classical name in the major Arabic and Turkish references. In some practical teaching circles, however, it appears to be used for Shawq Afza transposed to Rast (C).
short_identity: A non-standard label sometimes used in practical teaching for Shawq Afza on C, and not to be confused with Suznak, which is a different maqam altogether.

quick_facts.karar: Rast (C) in the practical reading that treats it as Shawq Afza on C
quick_facts.ghammaz: Upper region shaped by the Shawq Afza model
quick_facts.lower_jins: Practical explanation depends on the Shawq Afza-on-C reading rather than on a fully independent standard structure
quick_facts.upper_jins: Upper Hijaz-oriented material inherited from the Shawq Afza model
quick_facts.character: Non-standard label, used in some circles rather than a broadly codified classical maqam name
quick_facts.common_start: Best understood by referring it back to Shawq Afza, then imagining the same modal idea transposed to Rast (C)

sayr.summary: Suznal is not among the most standard classical maqam names in the major Arabic and Turkish theoretical sources. What could be established more reliably is that some modern practical or teaching sources use Suznal to mean Shawq Afza transposed to C. In that sense, the safest and most accurate way to present it is as a non-standard label used in some environments for Shawq Afza on Rast (C), rather than as a separate widely codified maqam in its own right.
resting_tones: Rast (C): the practical tonic if Suznal is understood as Shawq Afza on C
The upper region shaped by the Shawq Afza model remains structurally important
Its identity is derived, in this interpretation, from a transposed model rather than from an independent classical description
motion_notes: Its sayr should be understood through the Shawq Afza model rather than through a separately stabilized Suznal theory
If one means the practical teaching label Suznal, the safest explanation is ‘Shawq Afza on Rast (C)’
It should not be confused with Suznak, which is a different maqam of the Rast family
Because the label is non-standard, explanatory honesty is more important here than artificial precision
common_path: Refer the listener back to the structural idea of Shawq Afza
Preserve the upper Hijaz-oriented character associated with the model
Return to the tonic on Rast in the practical reading

common_modulations: If Suznal is used in a practical-teaching sense, it is best treated as Shawq Afza on C rather than as a separate classical maqam with a distinct theory
It should not be confused with Suznak, which is a standard and different maqam of the Rast family built around Rast with Hijaz on the ghammaz
Because the label is not broadly standardized, any practical explanation should remain clearly marked as such and not be overstated as a settled classical identity
